### Frame 5 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

## CONCEPT 08 — MONOCHROME RIOT (ALL-IVORY EDITORIAL)

Title: Pillar 2 — Editorial Bold × Pillar 1 — Modern Minimalist Luxe (ivory-to-colour ink-bloom reel)

Format: Transformation Editorial Reel / All-Ivory Studio (monochrome-to-colour-riot lane)

Premise: Aira stands in a pure all-ivory world — ivory set, ivory props, ivory wardrobe — and on a single breath a riot of saturated colour blooms through the white like ink in water, flooding her look and the backdrop in one travelling wave, while she stays the same composed her.

Setting: an all-ivory editorial studio — a seamless ivory cyclorama, ivory plinths and sculptural props, soft monochrome light, a polished pale floor; the whole white world flooding with bold ink-colour on the bloom.

Wardrobe: LOOK A — an all-ivory sculptural editorial set: a structured ivory blazer-dress over tailored ivory trousers with a high modest neckline, fully modest, pristine monochrome; LOOK B (on the ink bloom) — the same sculptural silhouette flooded with a bold saturated colour-riot (deep magenta, cobalt and emerald blooming through the fabric in a painterly ink-wash), fully modest, high-fashion and alive.

Hair (locked all 7 frames): AP — a sleek centre-part straightened fall, mirror-glossy and pin-straight to just past the shoulders, crisp blunt ends, colour locked to reference. The sleek straight fall is the constant thread through the colour bloom.

Eye Colour (locked): warm dark-brown irises with faint amber flecks near the pupil and a soft limbal ring — unchanging across every frame and both looks, a crisp twin catchlight throughout.

Makeup: an editorial base — LOOK A: a clean luminous ivory-toned skin, a soft neutral eye, a defined brow, a soft nude lip; flooding on the ink bloom to LOOK B: the same skin with a bold graphic colour-pop accent (a saturated colour-wash on the lid and a bold colour lip), real pores and fine vellus texture throughout (real skin).

Footwear: LOOK A — clean ivory sculptural heels; LOOK B — the same heels flooded bold colour; continuity-locked within each frame, shown clean in the establishing frame.

Accessories: one per look, never cluttered — LOOK A: a single ivory sculptural cuff; LOOK B: the cuff flooded a bold saturated colour plus a single colour-pop earring.

Movement (one impossible move): the ivory-to-colour ink bloom — on one slow breath a saturated colour-riot blooms through the all-ivory world like ink in water along a single travelling wavefront (wardrobe, footwear, accessories and backdrop all flooding from white to colour on the wave) while her face, straight hair and pose hold locked (locked transform keyframes: START Frame 3 = all ivory at the breath → END Frame 5 = full colour-riot settled, signature beat Frame 4).

Concept Line: “Color was always the point.”

Duration: 24 seconds | 7 frames

Mood: PURE/MINIMAL → BOLD/ALIVE

Composition: a centred editorial hero in pure ivory negative space, a painterly colour-ink bloom flooding the frame.

Grade: a high-key monochrome-to-colour grade — LOOK A pure clean ivory-on-ivory, flooding on the bloom to LOOK B a saturated painterly colour-riot — unified by her constant warm skin and the glossy straight hair; clean uncrushed shadows, full mid-tone, no harsh contrast, controlled saturation (rich, never garish).

Spoken line: lands Frame 6 — “Color was always the point.” (English, natural Indian accent)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic beds (a clean studio room tone, a soft breath, a fine ink-bloom whoosh) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a bold minimalist editorial bed, kept low under the spoken line, never full.

Identity safety: AIRA is an adult of 25; both looks fully modest; the mood is bold and editorial, never sexualized; eye colour, face and the sleek straight hair locked to reference.

MAKEUP: the editorial Look-A base — a clean luminous ivory-toned skin, a soft neutral eye, a defined brow, a soft nude lip with a tiny wet light-point; skin real with visible pores and fine vellus texture.

FOOTWEAR: the clean ivory sculptural heels, pristine, both feet grounded on the polished pale floor, a clean soft reflection beneath.

ACCESSORIES: the single ivory sculptural cuff catching a clean soft glint, the one statement, never cluttered.

HANDS & NAILS: the right hand resting soft at her sternum as if about to breathe, the left arm relaxed at her side, fingers naturally curved with relaxed nail-beds; short almond nails in a soft nude, neat cuticles, no white-knuckle tension.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: a calm composed micro-smile barely lifting the left corner, the brow soft and level, lips parted barely 1mm on a slow inhale, eyes cool and direct to lens with a quiet editorial poise at the outer eye.

BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: front-facing with the torso rotated about 5° to open the right shoulder, weight settled on the back leg with a 2° hip counter-tilt, front shoulder dropped, chin level, spine long and clean; alive and breathing (living stillness).

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) a strand-edge of the straight fall shifting a millimetre in the studio air; (2) one clean soft specular sliding slow down the ivory lapel; (3) a faint soft shadow shifting on the ivory cyclorama behind her; (4) the monochrome key flickering imperceptibly at the seamless edge; (5) chest rising on a soft shallow inhale; (6) an ivory-cuff glint travelling along the wrist as her hand rests at the sternum; (7) the fabric breathing with a micro-shift of sheen as her ribcage expands; (8) a barely-perceptible eyelash settle completing a slow blink.

LIGHTING: KEY — a soft clean editorial beauty key feathered from above-front shaping a gentle cheekbone light. FILL — a soft bounce below lifting the under-chin just clean (gentle clamshell). RIM — a clean soft rim skimming the straight hair to separate her from the ivory cyclorama. PRACTICAL — a soft even wash holding the ivory pure. SEPARATION — the cyclorama pulled deep so she reads about 1 stop up against the ivory. BOUNCE — a soft kick adding a clean catch to the ivory fabric.

FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16; medium-wide, thigh-up with footwear glimpsed low, 12% headroom, clean ivory negative space camera-left to receive the colour bloom that arrives later.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: A medium-wide framing holds Aira at about 12% headroom in the all-ivory monochrome editorial world, the opening look settled as the hook. Her torso is turned about five degrees to open the right shoulder in a near-frontal address, with her weight on the back leg over a soft hip counter-tilt and the front shoulder dropped a touch. The camera sits at a mid-wide distance with the figure centred and clean clean ivory negative space held camera-left to receive the transform that arrives later. She faces front to the lens, near-frontal and alive rather than profiled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm, f/2.2; focus split between her near eye and the resting hand so both read crisp; the cyclorama melting to a smooth ivory field; the soft gradient rendering as a clean bloom; gentle highlight roll-off, no clipping.

COMPOSITION REFERENCE: a bold editorial establishing hero — pure, minimal, scroll-stopping.

ATMOSPHERE: pure, minimal, charged — the clean ivory hush before the colour.

COLOR GRADE: pure clean ivory-on-ivory grade — soft uncrushed shadows, clean ivory highlights, the faintest warm skin to separate her, full mid-tone, zero grain, no harsh contrast, no muddy murk; her warm skin held consistent.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: the ivory set shows a real fine sculptural weave with a crisp tailored edge; the ivory cuff reads as real matte sculpture; real skin micro-texture with soft peach-fuzz catching the rim at the jaw; a faint clean studio haze in the key.

PHYSICS: gravity-true fabric drape and hair fall; the cuff glint obeys a single light source; haze drifts on slow convection; weight visibly grounded through both feet — no floating, no stiffness.

### Frame 1 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium-wide, thigh-up, subject fills about 55% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a single slow 8% push-in across the clip, otherwise locked-off and stable — one move only, no whip, no handoff.

LENS: 35mm, f/2.2, smooth ivory background fall-off.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–3.2s — Aira settles her weight onto the back leg and rests her right hand soft at her sternum as if to breathe; 3.2–6.0s — she lifts her cool gaze to the lens on a slow inhale as a strand-edge shifts in the studio air. One clean subject action, real physics throughout.

OBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–6.0s — one clean soft specular travels slowly down the ivory lapel, the ivory cuff glints softly as her hand rests, and a faint soft shadow shifts on the cyclorama behind her. Environmental motion only, no object pop-in, the world still pure ivory.

PHYSICS: real fabric weight and hair inertia, gravity-true; the specular tracks a single fixed key; haze drifts on slow convection; no unmotivated motion.

LIGHTING: soft clean editorial key with soft fill; a clean specular travelling on the ivory; the cyclorama held deep; a clean soft rim steady on the straight hair.

ENVIRONMENT: an all-ivory studio, an ivory cyclorama, ivory plinths, a polished pale floor.

AUDIO: diegetic only, no in-clip music. Ambient — a clean low studio room tone throughout. SFX — a soft fabric-settle at 0.8s (gentle attack, short tail), a faint cuff-tick at 3.4s, a delicate air-swell at 4.8s timed to the gaze-lift. Reaction — a soft audible breath at 3.0s. ### Frame 4 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: energized medium of the ivory-to-colour ink bloom (the hero move).

CAMERA MOVEMENT: the slow push eases into a smooth gentle orbit around her through the bloom (one flowing move) — the move that showcases the flood.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0. (Transform-anchor distance — matches Frames 3 and 5.)

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–1.5s — from Frame 3’s breath the colour blooms out through the ivory and her hand eases off the sternum; 1.5–4.2s — the ink-wash floods the look along its wavefront as colour races across the fabric and the straight hair catches travelling colour (the impossible beat, face clear and cleanly lit, identity locked); 4.2–6.0s — the bloom settles and the colour-riot resolves toward the Frame-5 reveal. Strict interpolation between locked keyframes; real ink-diffusion physics.

One coherent transform, no extra objects, no pop-in.

PHYSICS: colour blooms through fabric with believable capillary spread and inertia; the bloom obeys a single travelling wavefront; face and hands constant; no melting of anatomy.

LIGHTING: key tinting ivory-to-colour through the bloom, soft fill, clean colour-rim, live speculars racing along the colour-edges.

ENVIRONMENT: the studio flooding ivory-to-colour, fine colour-droplets sparkling along the wave.

VO — none (the line lands two frames on).

DURATION: 4 seconds (0:09–0:13 of 24s total).

LIP-SYNC: none.

### Frame 7 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

One clean subject action, real physics throughout.

## CONCEPT 09 — LIQUID METAL (CHROME + WATER)

Title: Pillar 9 — Architectural Futurist × Pillar 6 — Sculpted Active (liquid-chrome body-wrap reel)

Format: Transformation Futurist Reel / Dark Reflective Studio (chrome-pour lane)

Premise: Aira stands composed in a dark futurist studio and on a single beat liquid chrome pours down from above and wraps around her in one travelling spiral — sculpting a flowing chrome accent over her matte architectural set — while her face, high pony and pose hold locked, strong and fluid.

Setting: a dark futurist studio — a black reflective floor with a shallow water-skin, cool graphite walls, a single sculptural light bar, faint drifting vapour; mirror-still water and chrome reflections everywhere.

Wardrobe: LOOK A — a matte deep-charcoal architectural set: a structured graphite high-neck bodice over sculptural tailored wide trousers, high modest neckline, fully modest, clean matte; LOOK B (on the chrome pour) — the same set wrapped by a flowing liquid-chrome sash-and-collar that pours on and sets to a mirror-bright sculptural chrome detail, fully modest, architectural and powerful.

Hair (locked all 7 frames): AP — a high sleek wrapped ponytail, mirror-glossy and pulled clean off the face, the tail wrapped at the base, a crisp sharp silhouette, colour locked to reference. The high sleek pony is the constant thread through the chrome pour.

Eye Colour (locked): warm dark-brown irises with faint amber flecks near the pupil and a soft limbal ring — unchanging across every frame and both looks, a crisp cool catchlight throughout.

Makeup: an architectural base — LOOK A: a clean matte-luminous skin, a soft sculpted contour, a defined brow, a soft neutral lip; refining on the chrome pour to LOOK B: the same skin with a cool metallic accent (a fine chrome sheen on the lid and a cool satin lip), real pores and fine vellus texture throughout (real skin).

Footwear: LOOK A — sleek matte-graphite architectural heels; LOOK B — the same heels with a mirror-chrome heel detail set by the pour; continuity-locked within each frame, shown clean in the establishing frame.

Accessories: one per look, never cluttered — LOOK A: a single sculptural matte cuff; LOOK B: the cuff set mirror-chrome plus a single sculptural chrome collar piece.

Movement (one impossible move): the chrome-pour body wrap — on one beat liquid chrome pours from above and wraps around her in a single travelling spiral (over the matte set, setting to a sculptural chrome sash-collar-and-cuff) while her face, high pony and pose hold locked (locked transform keyframes: START Frame 3 = matte set as the chrome crests above → END Frame 5 = chrome wrap fully set, signature beat Frame 4).

Concept Line: “I flow. I don’t break.”

Duration: 24 seconds | 7 frames

Mood: COOL/SCULPTURAL → FLUID/POWERFUL

Composition: a centred architectural hero on a black water-skin floor, a single travelling chrome spiral wrapping the frame.

Grade: a cool futurist grade — LOOK A matte graphite-on-black, refining on the pour to LOOK B mirror-chrome speculars over the matte — unified by her constant warm skin and the glossy high pony; clean uncrushed shadows, full mid-tone, no harsh contrast, controlled cool saturation (rich, never garish).

Spoken line: lands Frame 6 — “I flow. I don’t break.” (English, natural Indian accent)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic beds (a low studio hum, a water-drip, a chrome-pour shimmer) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a cool architectural electronic bed, kept low under the spoken line, never full.

Identity safety: AIRA is an adult of 25; both looks fully modest; the mood is strong and architectural, never sexualized; eye colour, face and the high sleek pony locked to reference.

### Frame 3 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium, chest-up, subject and raised hand fill about 60% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a single slow 6% push-in tracking the raised hand (one flowing move), otherwise stable. (Transform-anchor distance — matches Frames 4 and 5.)

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0, smooth background fall-off.

One clean subject action, real physics throughout.

OBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–6.0s — the chrome crests above and the first chrome-tendrils gather at the shoulder; vapour drifts and the water-skin mirror-brightens at the edges. Environmental motion only, no pop-in.

PHYSICS: the chrome crests by real surface tension; tendrils gather on convection; gravity-true hands and fabric; no unmotivated motion.

LIGHTING: cool sculptural key with soft fill cresting chrome at the edges; a crisp rim firing the chrome-tendrils into mirror points; the light bar behind; vapour low.

### Frame 6 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium, chest-up, subject fills about 58% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a single slow 5% push-in on the line (one flowing move), otherwise locked and stable.

LENS: 35mm, f/2.2.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–1.2s — Aira shifts her weight with an easy 6° turn and opens her right hand in a relaxed strong gesture; 1.2–2.9s — she delivers “I flow. I don’t break.” to the lens with a strong knowing smile and natural lip-sync; 2.9–4.8s — she holds the alive architectural look as the chrome set settles. One clean subject action, real physics throughout.

OBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–4.8s — the chrome set sways gently with the shift, the chrome collar catches travelling light on real planes, and a mirror specular travels along the chrome sash; clean vapour drifts behind. Environmental and worn-object motion only, no pop-in.

PHYSICS: gravity-true chrome-set sway, pony swing and collar light on the shift; grounded weight; accurate speech mechanics for the line; no unmotivated motion.

LIGHTING: a clean cool-balanced key with soft fill; a crisp rim on the high pony and chrome shoulder; the light bar mirrored in the chrome; clean vapour behind.

ENVIRONMENT: the chrome-set studio, the cool mirror reflections.

## CONCEPT 10 — PAPER & PETALS (PAPER-ART)

Title: Pillar 8 — Romantic Ethereal × Pillar 2 — Editorial Bold (paper-fold-to-petal-burst reel)

Format: Transformation Paper-Craft Reel / Handmade Studio (origami-to-petal lane)

Premise: Aira stands in a delicate handmade paper world — folded-paper backdrop, paper flowers, paper props — and on a single breath the folded paper unfolds and bursts into a wave of soft real petals that wrap around her in one travelling arc, while her face, tousled hair and pose hold locked, soft and alive.

Setting: a soft paper-craft studio — a handcrafted folded-paper backdrop, paper flowers on slim stems, paper-sculpture props, a warm pastel wash, fine paper-dust in the light; the flat paper world blooming into real petals on the move.

Wardrobe: LOOK A — a structured paper-craft sculptural dress: matte pastel origami-pleated bodice over a sculptural pleated midi skirt, high modest neckline, long sleeve, fully modest, crisp folded edges; LOOK B (on the petal burst) — the same silhouette blooming with soft real-petal texture and warm petal-colour washing through the pleats, fully modest, romantic and alive.

Hair (locked all 7 frames): AP — a tousled shag with a soft curtain fringe, lived-in texture framing the face, gentle movement at the ends, colour locked to reference. The tousled shag is the constant thread through the petal burst.

Eye Colour (locked): warm dark-brown irises with faint amber flecks near the pupil and a soft limbal ring — unchanging across every frame and both looks, a soft warm catchlight throughout.

Makeup: a soft romantic base — LOOK A: a clean dewy skin, a soft rosy eye, a feathered brow, a soft petal-nude lip; warming on the petal burst to LOOK B: the same skin with a soft petal-flush accent (a warm rose wash on the lid and a soft rose lip), real pores and fine vellus texture throughout (real skin).

Footwear: LOOK A — soft pastel sculptural heels; LOOK B — the same heels with a soft petal-tint set by the burst; continuity-locked within each frame, shown clean in the establishing frame.

Accessories: one per look, never cluttered — LOOK A: a single paper-craft sculptural earring; LOOK B: the earring softened with a delicate real-petal hairpiece at the temple.

Movement (one impossible move): the paper-fold to petal burst — on one breath the folded-paper flowers unfold and burst into soft real petals that wrap around her in a single travelling arc (the paper backdrop blooming, petals washing colour through her pleats) while her face, tousled hair and pose hold locked (locked transform keyframes: START Frame 3 = folded paper as it begins to unfold → END Frame 5 = petal burst fully bloomed, signature beat Frame 4).

Concept Line: “Unfold. Then bloom.”

Duration: 24 seconds | 7 frames

Mood: SOFT/CRAFTED → BLOOMING/ALIVE

Composition: a centred romantic hero in a handmade paper world, a single travelling petal arc wrapping the frame.

Grade: a soft warm paper-craft grade — LOOK A matte pastel paper tones, warming on the burst to LOOK B soft real-petal colour washing through — unified by her constant warm skin and the tousled shag; clean uncrushed shadows, full mid-tone, no harsh contrast, controlled warm saturation (soft, never garish).

Spoken line: lands Frame 6 — “Unfold. Then bloom.” (English, natural Indian accent)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic beds (a soft room tone, a paper-rustle, a petal-flutter) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a soft warm acoustic-and-music-box bed, kept low under the spoken line, never full.

Identity safety: AIRA is an adult of 25; both looks fully modest; the mood is soft and romantic, never sexualized; eye colour, face and the tousled shag locked to reference.

### Frame 5 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium close, chest-up, subject and petal shoulder fill about 58% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: the orbit eases back to a slow 5% push-in as the petal bloom resolves (one flowing move), otherwise stable.

OBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–6.0s — the petal dress settles and the petal hem sways gently, a soft specular travels along the petal pleats, and the last petals drift down and fade; clean petal-dust drifts behind. Environmental motion only, no pop-in.

PHYSICS: gravity-true petal-dress drape and hair; petals settle on convection; face and hands continuous from the transform; no unmotivated motion.

LIGHTING: a clean warm-balanced key with soft fill; a soft rim skimming the tousled shag and petal shoulder; the backdrop held deep; clean petal-dust behind.

ENVIRONMENT: the petal-bloomed studio, last petals settling.

MASTER TRACK BRIEF (Concept 10 — full reel, anchored at the Frame 4 petal burst and the Frame 6 line): a soft warm acoustic-and-music-box bed — a gentle fingerpicked guitar over a soft music-box motif and a light heartbeat kick, built around one signature note that lands exactly on the petal burst, with a tender three-note melody that stays the constant “her.” Tempo gentle and even around 86–92 BPM, key warm-major, soft and alive. The bed warms with the unfolding paper (Frame 3), lands its signature note at its peak on the paper-fold to petal burst (Frame 4), holds the melody through the petal-bloom reveal (Frame 5), then ducks cleanly under the spoken line “Unfold. Then bloom.” (Frame 6) so the vocal sits forward, and resolves on a sustained note that cross-fades clean at Frame 7 so the reel loops seamlessly with no audible seam. Keep it under the diegetic room tone, paper-rustle and petal-flutter SFX — rich and present, never a full driving mix.

## CONCEPT 11 — FIRST DAY, NEW CITY (GRWM)

Title: Pillar 3 — Effortless Day × Pillar 7 — Street Edge (get-ready-with-me speed-wrap reel)

Format: Transformation GRWM Reel / Sunlit New-City Apartment (morning-to-ready speed-ramp lane)

Premise: It’s Aira’s first morning in a new city; in a single continuous speed-wrap she goes from a soft sleepy morning to a put-together effortless day look — the loungewear, the hair, the bag all resolving in one travelling ramp — while her face, half-up hair and pose hold locked, fresh and ready.

Setting: a bright sunlit new-city apartment — a sunlit window with a soft city view, a simple vanity mirror, a couple of half-unpacked moving boxes, a mug of coffee, warm morning light spilling across pale walls; the room shifting from sleepy morning to bright ready on the wrap.

Wardrobe: LOOK A — a soft morning loungewear set: an oversized modest knit sweater over relaxed lounge trousers, high modest neckline, fully modest, cozy and lived-in; LOOK B (on the speed-wrap) — an effortless smart-casual day look: a clean tailored blazer over a modest top and tapered trousers, high modest neckline, fully modest, put-together and bright.

Hair (locked all 7 frames): AP — a soft half-up style secured with a claw clip, a few face-framing pieces left loose, lived-in and easy, colour locked to reference. The half-up claw-clip is the constant thread through the speed-wrap.

Eye Colour (locked): warm dark-brown irises with faint amber flecks near the pupil and a soft limbal ring — unchanging across every frame and both looks, a bright warm catchlight throughout.

Makeup: an effortless base — LOOK A: a fresh bare-glow skin, a soft groomed brow, a touched balm lip; building on the speed-wrap to LOOK B: the same skin with a clean day-makeup accent (a soft neutral wash on the lid, a soft flush, a tinted lip), real pores and fine vellus texture throughout (real skin).

Footwear: LOOK A — cozy ribbed socks; LOOK B — clean white sneakers set by the wrap; continuity-locked within each frame, shown clean in the establishing frame.

Accessories: one statement per look, never cluttered — LOOK A: a soft scrunchie on the wrist; LOOK B: a slim crossbody bag plus small gold hoops, the claw clip carrying through.

Movement (one impossible move): the GRWM speed-getting-ready wrap — on one beat a single travelling speed-ramp wraps her from the sleepy morning loungewear to the ready day look (the knit resolving to the blazer, the socks to sneakers, the scrunchie to the crossbody and hoops, the room brightening) while her face, half-up hair and pose hold locked (locked transform keyframes: START Frame 3 = morning loungewear as the ramp begins → END Frame 5 = ready day look settled, signature beat Frame 4).

Concept Line: “New city. Let’s begin.”

Duration: 24 seconds | 7 frames

Mood: SLEEPY/SOFT → READY/BRIGHT

Composition: a centred warm hero in a sunlit new-city apartment, a single travelling speed-ramp wrapping the frame.

Grade: a warm sunlit grade — LOOK A soft sleepy morning tones, brightening on the wrap to LOOK B a crisp put-together day light — unified by her constant warm skin and the half-up claw-clip; clean uncrushed shadows, full mid-tone, no harsh contrast, controlled warm saturation (soft, never garish).

Spoken line: lands Frame 6 — “New city. Let’s begin.” (English, natural Indian accent)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic beds (a soft morning room tone, a coffee-pour, distant city sounds) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a warm upbeat indie-pop bed, kept low under the spoken line, never full.

Identity safety: AIRA is an adult of 25; both looks fully modest; the mood is fresh and warm, never sexualized; eye colour, face and the half-up claw-clip hair locked to reference.

### Frame 1 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

EYE COLOUR: warm dark-brown irises with faint amber flecks near the pupil and a soft limbal ring, a bright warm catchlight from the morning window, gaze direct to lens, locked to reference.

WARDROBE: the soft morning loungewear — the oversized modest knit sweater over relaxed lounge trousers with a high modest neckline, the knit catching one soft warm specular, cozy and lived-in, zero snags.

HAIR: the soft half-up style secured with a claw clip, a few face-framing pieces left loose, lived-in and easy, individual strands reading real, colour locked to reference.

MAKEUP: the effortless Look-A base — a fresh bare-glow skin, a soft groomed brow, a touched balm lip with a tiny wet light-point; skin real with visible pores and fine vellus texture.

FOOTWEAR: the cozy ribbed socks, soft and lived-in, both feet grounded on the warm apartment floor, a soft morning shadow beneath.

ACCESSORIES: the soft scrunchie on the wrist catching a soft warm glint, the one statement, never cluttered.

HANDS & NAILS: the right hand wrapped soft around a warm coffee mug at her chest, the left arm relaxed at her side, fingers naturally curved with relaxed nail-beds; short almond nails in a soft nude, neat cuticles, no white-knuckle tension.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: a calm sleepy micro-smile barely lifting the left corner, the brow soft and level, lips parted barely 1mm on a slow morning inhale, eyes warm and direct to lens with a gentle just-woke poise at the outer eye.

BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: front-facing with the torso rotated about 5° to open the right shoulder, weight settled on the back leg with a 2° hip counter-tilt, front shoulder dropped, chin level, spine long and easy; alive and breathing (living stillness).

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) a face-framing strand shifting a millimetre in the morning air; (2) one soft warm specular sliding slow down the knit sleeve; (3) a thread of coffee-steam curling up from the mug; (4) the window light flickering imperceptibly as a cloud passes; (5) chest rising on a soft shallow inhale; (6) a scrunchie shift on the wrist as her hand holds the mug; (7) the knit breathing with a micro-shift of weave as her ribcage expands; (8) a barely-perceptible eyelash settle completing a slow blink.

SPATIAL LOGIC: FOREGROUND — the mug and the wrist scrunchie at roughly 20% frame depth, tack sharp. MIDGROUND — Aira centred, three-quarter to full torso. BACKGROUND — the sunlit window with a soft city view and a couple of moving boxes behind her for clean separation, no clutter.

LIGHTING: KEY — a soft warm morning window key feathered from camera-left shaping a gentle cheekbone light. FILL — a soft warm bounce below lifting the under-chin just clean (gentle clamshell). RIM — a soft warm rim skimming the half-up hair to separate her from the window. PRACTICAL — the warm city window glow behind. SEPARATION — the window pulled deep so she reads about 1 stop up. BOUNCE — a warm kick off the pale wall adding a soft catch to the knit.

FRAMING: ASPECT 9:16; medium-wide, thigh-up with socks glimpsed low, 12% headroom, clean warm negative space camera-right to receive the speed-wrap that arrives later.
SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: A medium-wide framing holds Aira at about 12% headroom in the first-day-new-city GRWM world, getting ready by a bright window, the opening look settled as the hook. Her torso is turned about five degrees to open the right shoulder in a near-frontal address, with her weight on the back leg over a soft hip counter-tilt and the front shoulder dropped a touch. The camera sits at a mid-wide distance with the figure centred and clean clean warm negative space held camera-right to receive the transform that arrives later. She faces front to the lens, near-frontal and alive rather than profiled. ORIENTATION LOCK — preserve this exact left-to-right composition; do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

LENS + DOF + BOKEH: 35mm, f/2.2; focus split between her near eye and the mug so both read crisp; the window melting to a smooth warm field; the city view rendering as soft bokeh; gentle highlight roll-off, no clipping.

COMPOSITION REFERENCE: a warm GRWM establishing hero — sleepy, cozy, relatable.

ATMOSPHERE: soft, sleepy, charged — the warm morning hush before the day.

COLOR GRADE: soft warm sleepy-morning grade — soft uncrushed shadows, clean warm highlights, the faintest warm skin to separate her, full mid-tone, zero grain, no harsh contrast, no muddy murk; her warm skin held consistent.

TEXTURE & MICRO-DETAIL: the knit shows a real chunky weave with soft pilling at the cuff; the mug reads as real warm ceramic; real skin micro-texture with soft peach-fuzz catching the rim at the jaw; soft coffee-steam and fine dust-motes in the morning beam.

PHYSICS: gravity-true knit drape and hair fall; the scrunchie sits with real weight; coffee-steam rises on slow convection; weight visibly grounded through both feet — no floating, no stiffness.

NEGATIVE: mirrored, flipped, inverted, extra limbs, fused fingers, melted features, asymmetric eyes (unintentional), garbled text, floating shadows, duplicated subjects, low-res patches, plastic skin, over-smoothed skin, oversharpened, AI-generated artifacts, watermarks, signature, deformed hands, malformed teeth, joint cuts at ankle/knee/wrist/elbow/neck, amputated fingertips, cross-eyed, lazy eye, off-axis pupils, melted clothing, mismatched footwear, mannequin-stiff posture, dead eyes, statue-frozen energy, crushed muddy blacks, garish oversaturation, flat dull light, eye-colour drift, sexualization, de-aging.

### Frame 1 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium-wide, thigh-up, subject fills about 55% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: a single slow 8% push-in across the clip, otherwise locked-off and stable — one move only, no whip, no handoff.

LENS: 35mm, f/2.2, smooth warm background fall-off.

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–3.2s — Aira settles her weight onto the back leg and cradles the warm coffee mug soft at her chest; 3.2–6.0s — she lifts her warm sleepy gaze to the lens on a slow morning inhale as a face-framing strand shifts in the air. One clean subject action, real physics throughout.

OBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–6.0s — one soft warm specular travels slowly down the knit sleeve, coffee-steam curls up from the mug, and the window light shifts soft as a cloud passes. Environmental motion only, no object pop-in, still sleepy morning.

PHYSICS: real knit weight and hair inertia, gravity-true; the specular tracks a single fixed key; steam rises on slow convection; no unmotivated motion.

LIGHTING: soft warm morning window key with soft fill; a soft specular travelling on the knit; the window glow behind; a soft warm rim steady on the half-up hair.

ENVIRONMENT: a sunlit new-city apartment, a window with a city view, moving boxes, a coffee mug.

AUDIO: diegetic only, no in-clip music. Ambient — a soft morning room tone with faint distant city sounds throughout. SFX — a soft mug-ceramic settle at 0.8s (gentle attack, short tail), a faint coffee-sip swallow at 3.4s, a delicate air-swell at 4.8s timed to the gaze-lift. Reaction — a soft audible morning breath at 3.0s. The master track sits low — a warm indie-pop motif just kindling under the room tone (present, never full). VO — none this frame.

DURATION: 3 seconds (0:00–0:03 of 24s total).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps acquisition, 180° shutter, natural motion blur on the gaze-lift; playback 24fps real-time.

LIP-SYNC: none — lips closed/softly parted, composed.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, double-exposure unless intended, audio desync, lip-sync drift, wardrobe drift, unmotivated camera shake, physics violation (knit/hair/steam/light), mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker, eye-colour drift, sexualization, de-aging.

Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

## CONCEPT 12 — SANGEET NIGHT (FESTIVE GRWM)

Title: Pillar 4 — Indo-Modern Fusion × Pillar 5 — Evening Defined (fairy-light-bloom festive-wrap reel)

Format: Transformation Festive GRWM Reel / Sangeet Venue (getting-ready-to-festive fairy-light lane)

Premise: Aira is getting ready for a sangeet night; on a single beat strings of warm fairy lights bloom and wrap around her in one travelling spiral — the simple getting-ready set resolving into a full Indo-modern festive look — while her face, braided low bun and pose hold locked, warm and radiant.

Setting: a festive sangeet venue at dusk turning to night — marigold garlands, warm string-light canopies, soft drapes, a decorated indoor-outdoor terrace, a warm golden glow deepening to festive night; the space blooming with fairy lights on the move.

Wardrobe: LOOK A — a simple getting-ready set: a soft modest cotton kurta over relaxed pants, high modest neckline, fully modest, pre-glam and calm; LOOK B (on the fairy-light bloom) — a full Indo-modern festive look: an embellished modest lehenga-and-blouse with a draped dupatta (or a sharara set), high modest neckline, full sleeve, fully modest, richly embroidered and radiant.

Hair (locked all 7 frames): AP — a sleek braided low bun with softly laid baby hairs framing the face, a few delicate flyaways, colour locked to reference. The braided low bun is the constant thread through the fairy-light bloom.

Eye Colour (locked): warm dark-brown irises with faint amber flecks near the pupil and a soft limbal ring — unchanging across every frame and both looks, a warm festive catchlight throughout.

Makeup: a festive base — LOOK A: a fresh dewy skin, a soft groomed brow, a touched balm lip; building on the fairy-light bloom to LOOK B: the same skin with a rich festive accent (a warm shimmer-bronze lid, a soft kohl line, a defined warm-rose lip), real pores and fine vellus texture throughout (real skin).

Footwear: LOOK A — simple flat slippers; LOOK B — embellished festive heels set by the bloom; continuity-locked within each frame, shown clean in the establishing frame.

Accessories: one statement per look, never cluttered — LOOK A: simple small studs; LOOK B: statement jhumka earrings plus a delicate maang tikka and a stack of fine bangles.

Movement (one impossible move): the fairy-light bloom wrap — on one beat warm fairy lights bloom and wrap around her in a single travelling spiral (the getting-ready set resolving to the festive look, the venue blooming with string lights) while her face, braided low bun and pose hold locked (locked transform keyframes: START Frame 3 = getting-ready set as the lights crest → END Frame 5 = festive look fully set, signature beat Frame 4).

Concept Line: “Tonight, we light it up.”

Duration: 24 seconds | 7 frames

Mood: WARM/SOFT → FESTIVE/RADIANT

Composition: a centred festive hero under warm string-light canopies, a single travelling fairy-light spiral wrapping the frame.

Grade: a warm festive grade — LOOK A soft pre-glam warm tones, blooming on the wrap to LOOK B rich golden festive light with warm bokeh — unified by her constant warm skin and the braided low bun; clean uncrushed shadows, full mid-tone, no harsh contrast, controlled warm saturation (rich, never garish).

Spoken line: lands Frame 6 — “Tonight, we light it up.” (English, natural Indian accent)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic beds (a soft venue tone, distant dhol-and-laughter, a fairy-light shimmer) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a warm festive Indo-pop bed, kept low under the spoken line, never full.

Identity safety: AIRA is an adult of 25; both looks fully modest; the mood is warm and festive, never sexualized; eye colour, face and the braided low bun locked to reference.

## CONCEPT 13 — 5 AM CLUB (SUNRISE GRWM)

Title: Pillar 3 — Effortless Day × Pillar 6 — Sculpted Active (sunrise-sweep morning-wrap reel)

Format: Transformation Sunrise GRWM Reel / Minimalist Apartment (pre-dawn-to-active sunrise-sweep lane)

Premise: It’s 5 AM and Aira rises before the sun; on a single beat a travelling sunrise-light sweep rolls across the room and wraps her from a sleepy pre-dawn moment to a bright active morning-ready look — the loungewear, the room, the light all resolving in one continuous sweep — while her face, messy top knot and pose hold locked, calm and alive.

Setting: a clean minimalist apartment at 5 AM — pre-dawn blue dark giving way to a warm sunrise glow through a wide window, a rolled yoga mat, a water bottle, simple plants, bare pale floors; the room sweeping from pre-dawn to sunrise on the move.

Wardrobe: LOOK A — soft modest sleep-loungewear: an oversized modest long-sleeve tee over relaxed lounge shorts-over-leggings, high modest neckline, fully modest, sleepy and soft; LOOK B (on the sunrise sweep) — a modest active set: full-coverage leggings with a loose modest long-line sports top and a light zip layer, high modest neckline, fully modest, clean and ready to move.

Hair (locked all 7 frames): AP — a soft messy top knot with a few loose tendrils framing the face, lived-in and easy, colour locked to reference. The messy top knot is the constant thread through the sunrise sweep.

Eye Colour (locked): warm dark-brown irises with faint amber flecks near the pupil and a soft limbal ring — unchanging across every frame and both looks, a soft catchlight cooling pre-dawn then warming at sunrise.

Makeup: a no-makeup-makeup base — LOOK A: a fresh bare skin, a soft groomed brow, a touched balm lip; building on the sunrise sweep to LOOK B: the same skin with a clean fresh-faced active glow (a soft tinted balm, a groomed brow, a healthy dewy sheen), real pores and fine vellus texture throughout (real skin).

Footwear: LOOK A — barefoot on the bare floor; LOOK B — clean training shoes set by the sweep; continuity-locked within each frame, shown clean in the establishing frame.

Accessories: one statement per look, never cluttered — LOOK A: a soft hair-tie on the wrist; LOOK B: a sleek smartwatch plus a clean water bottle in hand.

Movement (one impossible move): the sunrise sweep across the room — on one beat a single travelling sunrise-light sweep rolls across the room and wraps her from the sleepy loungewear to the active morning look (the tee resolving to the active set, the barefoot to training shoes, the room brightening pre-dawn-to-sunrise) while her face, messy top knot and pose hold locked (locked transform keyframes: START Frame 3 = loungewear as the sweep begins → END Frame 5 = active look settled, signature beat Frame 4).

Concept Line: “5 AM. This is my hour.”

Duration: 24 seconds | 7 frames

Mood: PRE-DAWN/STILL → SUNRISE/ALIVE

Composition: a centred calm hero in a minimalist apartment, a single travelling sunrise-light sweep crossing the frame.

Grade: a pre-dawn-to-sunrise grade — LOOK A cool blue pre-dawn tones, sweeping on the move to LOOK B a warm bright sunrise light — unified by her constant warm skin and the messy top knot; clean uncrushed shadows, full mid-tone, no harsh contrast, controlled saturation (soft, never garish).

Spoken line: lands Frame 6 — “5 AM. This is my hour.” (English, natural Indian accent)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic beds (a soft pre-dawn room tone, faint birdsong, a water-bottle set) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a calm building electronic-acoustic bed, kept low under the spoken line, never full.

Identity safety: AIRA is an adult of 25; both looks fully modest; the mood is calm and active, never sexualized; eye colour, face and the messy top knot locked to reference.

---

### Frame 1 of 7 — IMAGE PROMPT

IDENTITY LOCK: Aira [paste identity-lock reference sheet here].

MAKEUP: the no-makeup Look-A base — a fresh bare skin, a soft groomed brow, a touched balm lip with a tiny wet light-point; skin real with visible pores and fine vellus texture.

FOOTWEAR: barefoot on the bare pale floor, both feet grounded, a soft cool shadow beneath.

ACCESSORIES: the soft hair-tie on the wrist catching a soft cool glint, the one statement, never cluttered.

### Frame 1 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

NEGATIVE: mirrored, flipped, inverted, extra limbs, fused fingers, melted features, asymmetric eyes (unintentional), garbled text, floating shadows, duplicated subjects, low-res patches, plastic skin, over-smoothed skin, oversharpened, AI-generated artifacts, watermarks, signature, deformed hands, malformed teeth, joint cuts at ankle/knee/wrist/elbow/neck, amputated fingertips, cross-eyed, lazy eye, off-axis pupils, melted clothing, mismatched footwear, mannequin-stiff posture, dead eyes, statue-frozen energy, crushed muddy blacks, garish oversaturation, flat dull light, visible loop seam, look mismatch with Frame 1, eye-colour drift, sexualization, de-aging.

### Frame 7 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

This is my hour.” (Frame 6) so the vocal sits forward, and resolves on a sustained chord that cross-fades clean at Frame 7 so the reel loops seamlessly with no audible seam. ## CONCEPT 14 — GLASS SKIN (BEAUTY / SERUM)

Title: Pillar 1 — Modern Minimalist Luxe × Pillar 8 — Romantic Ethereal (serum-glow glass-bloom beauty reel)

Format: Transformation Beauty Reel / Minimalist Beauty Set (dewy-to-luminous glass-bloom lane)

Premise: A beauty serum moment; on a single beat a travelling dewy glass-glow wave rolls over Aira’s skin and wraps a luminous glass-skin finish in one continuous bloom — the soft spa look, the skin, the light all resolving into a clean editorial glow — while her face, pin-straight hair and pose hold locked, soft and luminous.

Setting: a clean minimalist beauty set — a soft white-to-pastel seamless backdrop, a simple vanity with a glass serum bottle, soft diffused beauty light, fine water droplets and a dewy atmosphere; the set shifting from soft spa-dew to crisp luminous editorial on the move.

Wardrobe: LOOK A — a soft minimal modest spa wrap: a clean modest robe-style wrap top over relaxed lounge trousers, high modest neckline, fully modest, soft and dewy; LOOK B (on the glass bloom) — a sleek minimal modest luxe top: a clean structured modest top in soft ivory, high modest neckline, fully modest, editorial and crisp.

Hair (locked all 7 frames): AP — sleek pin-straight hair with a soft blunt micro-fringe, glossy and smooth, colour locked to reference. The pin-straight micro-fringe is the constant thread through the glass bloom.

Eye Colour (locked): warm dark-brown irises with faint amber flecks near the pupil and a soft limbal ring — unchanging across every frame and both looks, a clean soft catchlight throughout.

Makeup: a skin-first base — LOOK A: a soft dewy bare skin, a soft groomed brow, a touched balm lip; building on the glass bloom to LOOK B: the same skin elevated to a luminous glass-skin finish (a dewy lit-from-within glow, a soft groomed brow, a clean glossy nude lip), real pores and fine vellus texture throughout (real skin, never plastic).

Footwear: LOOK A — soft spa slippers; LOOK B — clean minimal heels set by the bloom; continuity-locked within each frame, shown clean in the establishing frame.

Accessories: one statement per look, never cluttered — LOOK A: a soft spa headband holding the fringe clear; LOOK B: delicate fine ear studs.

Movement (one impossible move): the serum-glow glass bloom — on one beat a single travelling dewy glass-glow wave rolls over her skin and wraps the luminous glass-skin finish (the spa wrap resolving to the editorial top, the skin blooming to a lit-from-within glow) while her face, pin-straight hair and pose hold locked (locked transform keyframes: START Frame 3 = spa look as the glow crests → END Frame 5 = glass-skin finish set, signature beat Frame 4).

Concept Line: “Glass skin. No filter.”

Duration: 24 seconds | 7 frames

Mood: SOFT/DEWY → LUMINOUS/CLEAR

Composition: a centred clean beauty hero on a soft seamless backdrop, a single travelling glass-glow wave crossing the frame.

Grade: a clean beauty grade — LOOK A soft dewy spa tones, blooming on the move to LOOK B a crisp luminous editorial light — unified by her constant warm skin and the pin-straight micro-fringe; clean uncrushed shadows, full mid-tone, no harsh contrast, controlled saturation (clean, never garish).

Spoken line: lands Frame 6 — “Glass skin. No filter.” (English, natural Indian accent)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic beds (a soft studio room tone, a serum-pump click, a fine water-droplet) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a clean minimal ambient-pop bed, kept low under the spoken line, never full.

Identity safety: AIRA is an adult of 25; both looks fully modest; the mood is clean and luminous, never sexualized; eye colour, face and the pin-straight micro-fringe locked to reference.

### Frame 3 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium, chest-up, subject and easing hand fill about 60% of the vertical frame.

One clean subject action, real physics throughout.

OBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–6.0s — the spa look refines toward the editorial top and the first glass-glow tendrils form at the cheek and jaw; water-droplets drift and the set cleans at the edges. Environmental motion only, no pop-in.

PHYSICS: the spa look refines by real cloth-and-light mechanics; glow tendrils wave on convection; gravity-true hands and wrap; no unmotivated motion.

LIGHTING: soft clean beauty key with soft fill cleaning at the edges; a clean rim firing the glow-tendrils into clean points; the backdrop glow cleaning brighter; droplets behind.

## CONCEPT 15 — INKED (WINGED-LINER EDITORIAL)

Title: Pillar 2 — Editorial Bold × Pillar 9 — Architectural Futurist (winged-liner ink-draw editorial reel)

Format: Transformation Editorial Reel / Graphic Studio (pre-glam-to-bold ink-draw lane)

Premise: A bold editorial beauty moment; on a single beat a travelling ink-stroke draws across the frame and wraps Aira into a striking winged-liner editorial look — the clean base, the liner, the architectural wardrobe all resolving as the ink draws — while her face, space buns and pose hold locked, sharp and graphic.

Setting: a bold editorial studio — a graphic dark seamless backdrop with one hard graphic key, a sweep of liquid ink as a motif, clean modern geometry, a stark crisp atmosphere; the set shifting from clean pre-glam to bold graphic on the move.

Wardrobe: LOOK A — a clean minimal modest base: a simple structured modest top in soft charcoal with a high modest neckline, fully modest, pre-glam and calm; LOOK B (on the ink draw) — a bold structured modest editorial look: an architectural modest top with sculptural shoulders in deep black, high modest neckline, full coverage, fully modest, graphic and striking.

Hair (locked all 7 frames): AP — two sleek high space buns with smooth laid edges and a clean centre part, glossy and sharp, colour locked to reference. The space buns are the constant thread through the ink draw.

Eye Colour (locked): warm dark-brown irises with faint amber flecks near the pupil and a soft limbal ring — unchanging across every frame and both looks, a crisp graphic catchlight throughout.

Makeup: an editorial base — LOOK A: a clean matte-natural skin, a soft groomed brow, a touched balm lip; building on the ink draw to LOOK B: the same skin with a bold graphic accent (a crisp jet-black winged liner, a defined brow, a soft matte berry lip), real pores and fine vellus texture throughout (real skin).

Footwear: LOOK A — clean minimal flats; LOOK B — sharp architectural heels set by the draw; continuity-locked within each frame, shown clean in the establishing frame.

Accessories: one statement per look, never cluttered — LOOK A: a single fine stud; LOOK B: a bold sculptural ear cuff.

Movement (one impossible move): the winged-liner ink draw — on one beat a single travelling ink-stroke draws across the frame, drawing her crisp winged liner and wrapping the bold editorial look (the base resolving to the architectural top, the set sharpening to graphic) while her face, space buns and pose hold locked (locked transform keyframes: START Frame 3 = clean base as the ink crests → END Frame 5 = bold winged-liner look set, signature beat Frame 4).

Concept Line: “I draw my own line.”

Duration: 24 seconds | 7 frames

Mood: STARK/POISED → BOLD/GRAPHIC

Composition: a centred graphic hero on a dark seamless backdrop, a single travelling ink-stroke drawing across the frame.

Grade: a bold editorial grade — LOOK A clean stark neutral tones, drawing on the move to LOOK B a bold high-contrast graphic light with deep blacks — unified by her constant warm skin and the space buns; clean uncrushed shadows, full mid-tone, controlled contrast (bold but not crushed), controlled saturation (graphic, never garish).

Spoken line: lands Frame 6 — “I draw my own line.” (English, natural Indian accent)

Audio note: per-frame diegetic beds (a clean studio tone, an ink-brush stroke, a soft set hum) plus a separate Master Track Brief — a bold minimal electronic bed, kept low under the spoken line, never full.

Identity safety: AIRA is an adult of 25; both looks fully modest; the mood is bold and graphic, never sexualized; eye colour, face and the space buns locked to reference.

FACIAL MICRO-DYNAMICS: a bold confident smile lifting both cheeks, the left corner a hair higher, eyes shining direct to lens with a crisp graphic catchlight, an alive bold poise at the outer eye — the graphic resolve of the draw.

BODY POSTURE & WEIGHT: front-facing, torso rotated about 4° opening the right shoulder, weight settled easy on the back leg with a 2° hip counter-tilt, chin level, spine long, the whole stance bold and graphic (living stillness).

KINETIC STILLNESS (8 layers): (1) an edge-strand drifting slow at the temple; (2) the last ink-sheen settling with a slow crisp glint; (3) the sculptural shoulder breathing soft as she settles; (4) clean graphic light drifting around her; (5) chest rising on a slow inhale; (6) a crisp specular sliding along the sculptural-edge as she settles; (7) the ear cuff catching a micro-glint; (8) a barely-perceptible eyelash settle completing a slow blink.

BACKGROUND — the dark graphic backdrop softly defocused with a clean gradient behind her head, clean separation, no clutter.

RIM — a crisp rim skimming the space buns and the sculptural shoulder to separate her from the backdrop. PRACTICAL — the graphic glow now bold. SEPARATION — the backdrop pulled deep so she reads cleanly against the graphic light. BOUNCE — a controlled kick off a card on the skin.

SUBJECT FRAMING & POSITION: A medium close framing holds Aira at about 11% headroom, the settling hand entering the lower third within the winged-liner inked editorial world. Her torso is turned about five degrees to open the right shoulder in a near-frontal address, with her weight on the back leg over a soft hip counter-tilt and the front shoulder dropped a touch. The camera holds an intimate beauty distance with the figure centred and clean clean graphic negative space held camera-right. She faces front to the lens, composed and alive in the beat. NEGATIVE: mirrored, flipped, inverted, extra limbs, fused fingers, melted features, asymmetric eyes (unintentional), garbled text, floating shadows, duplicated subjects, low-res patches, plastic skin, over-smoothed skin, oversharpened, AI-generated artifacts, watermarks, signature, deformed hands, malformed teeth, joint cuts at ankle/knee/wrist/elbow/neck, amputated fingertips, cross-eyed, lazy eye, off-axis pupils, melted clothing, mismatched footwear, mannequin-stiff posture, dead eyes, statue-frozen energy, crushed muddy blacks, garish oversaturation, flat dull light, eye-colour drift, sexualization, de-aging.

### Frame 5 of 7 — VIDEO PROMPT

IDENTITY & CHARACTER-CONSISTENCY LOCK: render Aira strictly from the locked identity reference and hold her the SAME PERSON for the entire 6-second clip and every generated frame. Treat identity as a FIXED skeleton that never changes — bone structure (skull, cheekbones, jawline, chin), overall face shape and proportions, eye shape, spacing and iris colour, nose and lip shape, skin tone and real skin texture (visible pores, fine peach-fuzz; no plastic smoothing, no beautification creep, no face re-shaping), hairline, hair colour and texture, height, build and hand/finger anatomy. ONLY the soft expression muscles may move — eyelids, brows, cheeks and mouth corners — together with the eyes, gaze and head; expression and lip-sync animate ON TOP of that fixed identity and must NEVER alter the face shape, the eye colour, or who she is. Keep expression changes smooth and moderate and lead them with the EYES and GAZE (soft smiles, brow lifts, a parted breath, eyes tracing the object) — no extreme grimace, no wide-mouth distortion, no hard squint, no rapid expression flicker that would warp the geometry; favour one clean expression transition per clip. ABSOLUTE ZERO identity drift: no morphing, melting, smearing, rubbery warping, face-swapping, twinning or a second/duplicate subject, no age, weight, ethnicity or gender shift, no extra, missing or fused fingers or limbs. Identity stays identical at the first frame, every in-between frame, the loop seam and the last frame. Through any transform, impossible move or fast action ONLY the intended element (wardrobe, environment, props or effect) changes — her face, identity and proportions stay fully recognizable; interpolate strictly between the locked keyframes and never invent a new face, new hands, extra limbs or a second subject. Do not mirror, flip or invert the frame.

SHOT TYPE: medium close, chest-up, subject and sculptural shoulder fill about 58% of the vertical frame.

CAMERA MOVEMENT: the orbit eases back to a slow 5% push-in as the bold look resolves (one flowing move), otherwise stable.

LENS: 50mm, f/2.0. (Transform-anchor distance — matches Frames 3 and 4.)

SUBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–2.8s — the draw settles as Aira reaches to settle the sculptural shoulder with an easy roll of the shoulder; 2.8–6.0s — a bold confident smile eases to the lens on a slow breath as the last ink-sheen settles. One clean subject action, real physics throughout.

OBJECT ACTION WITH TIMING: 0.0–6.0s — the bold look settles and the sculptural shoulder sways gently, a crisp specular travels along the sculptural-edge and the ear cuff catches a glint, and the last ink-sheen drifts and fades; clean graphic light drifts behind. Environmental motion only, no pop-in.

PHYSICS: gravity-true architectural-top drape and hair; ink-sheen settles on convection; face and hands continuous from the transform; no unmotivated motion.

LIGHTING: a crisp graphic key with soft controlled fill; a crisp rim skimming the space buns and sculptural shoulder; the graphic glow bold; clean graphic light behind.

ENVIRONMENT: the bold editorial set, last ink-sheen settling.

AUDIO: diegetic only. Ambient — a clean studio room tone with faint set hum. SFX — a soft fabric-settle rustle at 1.0s, a delicate ear-cuff tick at 2.8s, a faint clean air-ease at 5.2s (gentle attack, long soft tail). Reaction — a soft slow breath at 3.2s. The master track holds — a bold minimal electronic groove settling under the room tone (present, never full). VO — none (the line lands next frame).

DURATION: 3 seconds (0:13–0:16 of 24s total).

FRAME RATE + MOTION BLUR: 24fps acquisition with a touch of slow-mo on the settling shoulder, 180° shutter, gentle natural motion blur; playback real-time.

LIP-SYNC: none.

NEGATIVE: morphing, warping, identity drift, limb teleport, object pop-in, frame skip, mirrored composition, text overlay, watermark, AI artifact, ghosting, double-exposure unless intended, audio desync, lip-sync drift, wardrobe drift, unmotivated camera shake, physics violation (architectural-top/hair/cuff/light), mannequin posture, frozen background, identity flicker, eye-colour drift, sexualization, de-aging.
